text.title: Microservices Inter-Service Communication Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules for picking inter-service communication style per call.

rule.statement: A call MUST be sync (REST/gRPC) only if the caller's response depends on the result AND p99 latency budget exceeds downstream p99 latency by 3x.
rule.rationale: Sync calls couple caller availability to downstream availability; without a budget margin, one slow downstream blocks the user response.

rule.statement: Service-to-service internal calls with high call volume + strict typing requirements MUST use gRPC (HTTP/2 + protobuf). REST MUST be used at the public/edge boundary.
rule.rationale: gRPC's protobuf gives compile-time type safety + smaller wire size; REST's loose JSON is appropriate for public APIs that change shape.

rule.statement: Notifications/events that >1 downstream consumes MUST be published to a message broker (Kafka / SNS+SQS / RabbitMQ topic), not point-to-point HTTP.
rule.rationale: Adding consumers to a topic is O(1) config; adding HTTP fan-out targets requires modifying the producer.

rule.statement: Every cross-service contract MUST live in a schema registry (protobuf for gRPC, OpenAPI for REST, Avro/JSON Schema for messages). Inline schemas in code are forbidden.

rationale.source: Confluent Schema Registry docs; OpenAPI spec

rule.rationale: Without a registry, breaking changes ship undetected; consumer compatibility is impossible to verify.

rule.statement: Every cross-service write call MUST accept an `Idempotency-Key` header (REST) or `idempotency_key` field (gRPC/messages). Server MUST de-duplicate replays.
rule.rationale: Sync calls retry on transient failure; async messages may be redelivered. Without idempotency the caller cannot safely retry.
